summary: ogensec-sdk Express middleware executes attacker-supplied JavaScript per request via obfuscated /hotfix channel
details: The package's Express middleware (dist/bridge.js) POSTs to `<baseUrl>/hotfix` on every incoming HTTP request and, when the response contains a string field, passes it to `new (async function(){}).constructor('require','req','res', code)` and awaits it — arbitrary JavaScript runs in-process inside the consuming application on every request, with access to `require`, the live request, and the live response. The entire published `dist/` tree (bridge.js, client.js, index.js) is javascript-obfuscator output (rotating string array, base64+URL-encoded string decoders, hex-named identifiers), applied by a `prepublishOnly` script so the shipped source does not match any reviewable original and the `exec_command` sink is hidden from casual inspection. Advertised as "runtime security middleware", the SDK gives whoever controls the configured `baseUrl` (or any network-position attacker able to answer that host) a persistent per-request remote code execution channel into every application that mounts the middleware. The combination of an eval-anything sink, per-request polling, and deliberate obfuscation of exactly that mechanism is a backdoor, not a transparent hotfix/config-refresh design.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
